0.name: hall of the great council
description: If previous rooms weren't enough, you still weren't prepared for this.
The plaque reads:"Hall of the Great Council replica, Doge's Palace"
While the hall itself is a masterpiece, as majestic as you could imagine with the largest oil painting on canvas in the world, what's inside that is truly beyond belief.
This hall is used as an assembly line for microprocessors and androids!
Production is certainly not mass-scale, but enough for prototypes.
All machinery exactly matches the style of the rest of the hall!
And while it's big for a room, it's not so big for a factory!
So, whoever designed this, crammed every single space.
Just staring at all this gives you a headache
It's like mixing Escher architecture with Ranaissance art, but is real!

1.name: hall of mirrors
description: The plaque says: Hall of Mirrors replica, Palace of Versailles.
You are stunned at what you are seeing.
The room's magnificence is beyond comprehension.
There are just a few candles, but the masterly placemente of mirror creates a dim, even light.
Each chandelier costs probably more than your home and there are lots of them!
The statues, the arches, the painted cellar... You could go on for a day and not even grasp a fraction of all that majesty.
You wonder if the person who did this is a madman or a genius.

2.name: dark corridor
description: Although it is just a corridor, the atmosphere is daunting!
Candlelight is dim and the walls are carved with complex patterns
Even if it's large, the effect is quite claustrophobic.
There's not much to see and you are glad you can leave that place.

3.name: gothic lab
description: This is another lab, but it is unlike any you have ever seen.
The room itself could be the most beautiful you can find in a castle but there are all kinds of instrumentation.
There are tools like abaci and stills, but also super hi tech photolithography tools, a server farm, and even a quantum computer and many other things you don't know.
You wonder what kind of technology is used here since old and new are intertwined for God knows what purpose.
To make it all even more surreal, there are paintings on the walls and statues in the corners, you doubt those are just replicas...

4.name: gothic passage
description: This is a passage room, but one you'd expect in a late medieval castle, with Gothic arches in every direction.

5.name: chapel
description: This room seems to be taken from another time. The plaque explains that it is an exact replica of the Chapel of St. John the Evangelist.
The original is in the White Tower of the Tower of London and is the oldest surviving complete chapel from the early Norman period.
You can say what you want, but you have to admit whoever did this has a good taste.

6.name: ceo office
description: The plaque simply reads:"CEO office". This room, in stark contrast to the other rooms in that wing, is relatively somber and minimalistic.
It prioritizes functionality but the materials are top tiers and there's a billiard table.
Maybe it looks a bit underwhelming compared to previous halls, but it looks like the perfect office.

7.name: crossroad
description: This is a crossway. To the north, the exit is a Gothic arch
To the west there's a door with a keypad.

8.name: south corridor
description: This is a pretty standard corridor. Not much to see here, except for the replicas of the most famous Renaissance paintings.

9.name: laboratory
description: This area is completely different from the rest, it's perfectly clean, painted white and with harsh fluorescent lighting.
There are materials like reagents and folders. Everything is meticulously organized and closed in lockers.

10.name: advanced lab
description: Maintaining the same clinical style of the previous room, this one has a workstation, a table and some other tools you don't recognize but seems to be hi tech.

11.name: sterile room
description: Another immaculate room, mostly empty except for hazard signs and numerous wall vents.

12.name: storeroom
description: In spite of being a storeroom, it is pretty empty. It looks like it has been a while since anyone last entered this place.

13.name: armor room
description: This room is unlike the rest. Its walls are way thicker, there are lots of cables, measuring instruments, stethoscopes, electroencephalographs.
In the middle there's a sort armor stand.
The stand itself is a work of art, wtch richly inlaid decorations.

14.name: main hall
description: This is the main hall, but in truth it's a theater. And what a theater!

The plaque explains that this is a replica of the Teatro Olimpico in Vicenza designed by Andrea Palladio which is the oldest indoor theater still in existence and is a masterpiece from the Renaissance era.
The rear corridor connects to the other rooms and is a masterpiece on its own adorned with paintings and small sculptures.

15.name: lobby
description: This is Megacorp lobby. It's really opulent and well represents company riches.
It's modern and somewhat minimalistic, boasting colours ranging from creamy pastel to gold.
There are paintings of medieval knights and even a statue of a rearing horse with a knight wielding a jousting lance and a shield, wich represents company logo.
It seems to be made of solid gold.
There's no one in the reception and this looks quite suspicious.

16.name: it room
description: This is the IT room. Unlike the theater, this is rather bland and somewhat messy
There are some computers and a couple of racks, one dedicated to the theater.
All is rather unimpressive, and the fact that this is a passage room speaks volumes about the consideration that Megacorp has of the IT department.

17.name: manager room
description: You enter the area manager's office, the person that likely is charge of that section of the building.
At a quick glance it seems bombastic but soon you realize that most of the materials are quite cheap.
All lacks coherence: on one side there's a replica of the Lady with an Ermine, on another a print of Escher, and on a third the poster of Raging Bull
It looks like it needs to satisfy at least three personalities!

18.name: bar
description: This is the recreational bar of Megacorp!
Dim lights, brass-inlaid shelves, red finishes, golden details...
It features a vast collection of super expensive spirits, from aged whiskeys to old rums, craft gins and so on.
It's excessive from every point of view.
While the theater was elegant and somber, this one seems came straight out from Shining!

19.name: training room
description: This area is larger than most, hosting several training dummies of various types.
One is called "The Fortress" and must have been extremely sturdy.
At least, judging by the pieces.
Something cut it in half so cleanly that the only things you can imagine doing that are a Jedi or a Sith with a lightsaber.
Whatever they are testing here, you really don't want to be the subject.

20.name: changing room
description: This is a standard changing room, except for the tools dedicated to the maintenance of weapons and armor.

21.name: treasury
description: Although the room is called the Treasury, it's rather bare. You see a plaque explaining how Megacorp created the ultimate HEMA weapon called Excalibur. It far exceeded expectations and is considered a weapon of war.
Behind the plaque there's a rock with a slot, as deep as half a sword blade's length.

22.name: red room
description: It's just an alcove with a dim, red, light.

23.name: blue room
description: It's just an alcove with a dim, blue, light.

24.name: north corridor
description: This is a pretty standard corridor. Not much to see here, except for the replicas of the most famous Renaissance sculptures.
